audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for ten virgins, talents, watchfulness, Son of Humanity throne, sheep/goats, least ones, age-long correction/life.

verses.restored: Then the reign of the heavens will be like ten virgins who took their lamps and went out to meet the bridegroom.
Five of them were foolish and five wise.
The foolish took their lamps but did not take oil with them.
The wise took oil in vessels with their lamps.
As the bridegroom delayed, they all became drowsy and slept.
At midnight there was a cry: Look, the bridegroom! Come out to meet him.
Then all those virgins rose and trimmed their lamps.
The foolish said to the wise, Give us some of your oil, because our lamps are going out.
The wise answered, Perhaps there will not be enough for us and you; go rather to the sellers and buy for yourselves.
While they were going away to buy, the bridegroom came; those ready went in with him to the wedding feast, and the door was shut.
Later the other virgins came also, saying, Lord, Lord, open to us.
But he answered, Amen, I say to you: I do not know you.
Therefore keep watch, because you know neither the day nor the hour.
For it is like a man going away who called his own servants and handed over his possessions to them.
To one he gave five talents, to another two, to another one, each according to his own ability; and he went away.
Immediately the one receiving five talents went and worked with them and gained five more.
Likewise the one with two gained two more.
But the one receiving one went away, dug in the ground, and hid his lord's silver.
After much time, the lord of those servants came and settled account with them.
The one receiving five talents brought five more, saying, Lord, you handed me five talents; look, I gained five more.
His lord said to him, Well done, good and faithful servant. You were faithful over a few things; I will appoint you over many things. Enter into the joy of your lord.
The one with two talents also came, saying, Lord, you handed me two talents; look, I gained two more.
His lord said to him, Well done, good and faithful servant. You were faithful over a few things; I will appoint you over many things. Enter into the joy of your lord.
The one having received one talent came and said, Lord, I knew you to be a hard man, reaping where you did not sow and gathering where you did not scatter.
Being afraid, I went and hid your talent in the ground. Look, you have what is yours.
His lord answered, Harmful and lazy servant, you knew that I reap where I did not sow and gather where I did not scatter?
Then you should have placed my silver with the bankers, and coming I would have received what was mine with interest.
Therefore take the talent from him and give it to the one having ten talents.
For to everyone having, more will be given and he will overflow; but from the one not having, even what he has will be taken.
Cast the unprofitable servant into the outer darkness; there will be weeping and grinding of teeth.
When the Son of Humanity comes in his glory, and all the messengers with him, then he will sit on his throne of glory.
All the nations will be gathered before him, and he will separate them from one another as the shepherd separates sheep from goats.
He will set the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.
Then the King will say to those on his right, Come, blessed of my Father, inherit the reign prepared for you from the foundation of the world.
For I was hungry and you gave me food; I was thirsty and you gave me drink; I was a stranger and you gathered me in;
naked and you clothed me; sick and you visited me; in prison and you came to me.
Then the righteous will answer, Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give drink?
When did we see you a stranger and gather you in, or naked and clothe you?
When did we see you sick or in prison and come to you?
The King will answer, Amen, I say to you: as much as you did it to one of the least of these my brothers and sisters, you did it to me.
Then he will say to those on the left, Depart from me, cursed ones, into the age-long fire prepared for the adversarial one and his messengers.
For I was hungry and you did not give me food; I was thirsty and you did not give me drink;
I was a stranger and you did not gather me in; naked and you did not clothe me; sick and in prison and you did not visit me.
Then they also will answer, Lord, when did we see you hungry, thirsty, a stranger, naked, sick, or in prison, and did not serve you?
Then he will answer, Amen, I say to you: as much as you did not do it to one of the least of these, neither did you do it to me.
These will go away into age-long correction, but the righteous into age-long life.
